questions.question: Explain the store-and-forward mechanism used in packet switching networks.
keyPoints: Definition: each router receives entire packet before forwarding
Storage in memory for error checking
Sequential processing through each node
Comparison with direct forwarding

questions.question: Compare datagram packet switching with virtual circuit packet switching.
keyPoints: Definition of each approach
Addressing differences (full address vs VCI)
Connection establishment (none vs pre-established path)
Routing decisions (per-packet vs path-based)
Examples (IP vs X.25/Frame Relay)

questions.question: List and explain the four types of delays that a packet experiences in a packet-switched network.
keyPoints: Processing delay - header examination and error checking
Queuing delay - waiting time in router buffers
Transmission delay - time to push bits onto link
Propagation delay - signal travel time through medium
Formula for total nodal delay

questions.question: What is Quality of Service (QoS) in packet-switched networks? Explain its significance.
keyPoints: Definition of QoS
Parameters: bandwidth, delay, jitter, packet loss
Applications requiring QoS: VoIP, video streaming
Mechanisms: queuing, traffic shaping, priority scheduling

questions.question: Explain the concept of packet fragmentation and when it becomes necessary.
keyPoints: Definition of fragmentation
MTU (Maximum Transmission Unit) concept
Reasons: different network segments have different MTU sizes
Process: breaking larger packet into smaller fragments
Reassembly at destination
Issues: overhead and potential further fragmentation

questions.question: What are the advantages of packet switching over circuit switching?
keyPoints: Efficient bandwidth utilization through dynamic sharing
Better resilience through alternate routing
Efficient handling of bursty traffic
Lower cost due to shared infrastructure
Scalability and flexibility

questions.question: Define jitter and explain how it affects real-time applications.
keyPoints: Definition: variation in packet arrival times
Causes: variable queuing delays at different routers
Effects on real-time applications: video playback issues, audio distortion in VoIP
Solutions: buffer playout, QoS mechanisms
